calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Julia Wald for and with Kurt Trutner (Administration).
John Curley and Mrs. Curley also present.

Ms. Wald provided courtesy copies of the supplement and a corrected order. Mr. Curley praised Ms. Wald for the services she provided and asked the Court to please bring this to a close. Ms. Wald stated that she did not understand that there was a problem, that she submitted a revised order not showing the stock values and that she provided the cash amounts that everyone will receive. Ms. Wald also stated that two of the Beneficiaries received a $25,000.00 preliminary distribution, that the third Beneficiary opted not to receive it, that he now wants it and that she will take what ever fees the Court awards. In addition, Ms. Wald stated that her Attorney license expires at the end of the year and that she will not be renewing it. The Second Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Continued To Dec-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Julia Wald for and with Kurt Trutner (Administrator).
Ellen Kerley (Beneficiary) also present.
John Kerley (Ellen's Husband) also present.

The Court noted that there is no endorsed copy of the Client's verified statement showing the correct proposed distribution to the heirs and that an itemization of the attorney costs is needed. Ms. Wald stated that she was unable to file her papers ahead of time, that it is her understanding that if fees are paid to other Counsel and it is described in the petition that a separate petition is not required, that she disagrees with the Probate Examiner that the refund for the advisory fee should not be counted as a receipt and that it took the Administrator a significant amount of time to get that back. In addition, Ms. Wald stated that the Brother elected not to receive a preliminary distribution at the time his Sisters did, that he now would like to receive his $25,000.00 distribution and asked the Court to approve it at this time. The Court denied Ms. Wald's request. The Second Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to December 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.
